Notice Of Motion To: (1) Compel Arbitration And (2) Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Aug-02-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Brett H. Oberst of Doll Amir & Eley LLP (310-557-9100) appears telephonically as counsel for defendant/non-opposing party, Capital One N.A.

No other appearance is made in this matter

DEFENDANT FIDELITY BROKERAGE SERVICES LLC'S Motion To: (1) Compel Arbitration And (2) Stay Proceedings is DENIED for each of three independently sufficient reasons. First, movant Fidelity Brokerage Services LLC is not a party to the action and does not demonstrate that the wrong parties were sued; attachments to the Evans declaration show there is in fact an entity named FMR LLC. Second, the only evidence supporting the motion is the Evans declaration, which is inadmissible for failure to comply with CCP 2015.5 ("under penalty of perjury under the laws of the State of California"). Third, plaintiff's declaration that he was never provided an arbitration agreement and never agreed to arbitrate is unrebutted. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR #11332, Bus. Tel: 415-551-3745. (D302)
